abstract:
This article presents key excerpts from the seminal 1947 work by Nikolai A. Voznesenskiy, "The Military Economy of the USSR during the Patriotic War." As a prominent Soviet statesman and Doctor of Economics, Voznesenskiy stood at the helm of the nation's industrial mobilization during World War II. Under his leadership, the Soviet Union designed and executed complex military-economic plans that shifted the country’s industrial heartland to the East, establishing a massive production base in the Urals, Siberia, and Central Asia that proved decisive in outproducing the Axis powers.

The text details the extraordinary measures taken to restructure the national economy for "total war," including the evacuation of over 1,300 major enterprises and the rapid expansion of the tank, aircraft, and armament industries. Beyond his role in weaponry production, Voznesenskiy was a primary figure in the Committee for the Restoration of the National Economy, overseeing the grueling process of rebuilding infrastructure, agriculture, and housing in territories liberated from Nazi occupation. These excerpts provide a data-rich, firsthand perspective on how the Soviet command economy adapted to catastrophic territorial losses to eventually achieve technical and quantitative superiority on the battlefield.
